summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO provider dispatch API/state handling must not drift between Claude, Codex, and custom provider assumptions in demand evidence mode.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
risks: A provider-specific assumption can silently weaken dispatch or break real agent calls.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch system implements provider-specific configuration with boundary enforcement and tool capability preservation for Claude, Codex, and custom providers.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime executes evidence agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) with provider-specific config and boundary auditing.
evidence.why: Core evidence dispatch implementation that spawns provider agents and validates boundary constraints.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs provider-specific CLI arguments for Claude, Codex, and custom providers with isolated model and sandbox config.
evidence.why: Provider invocation logic prevents cross-provider configuration leakage.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots detect project file mutations before/after evidence agent execution using git-backed file tracking.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement detects any project mutations outside allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentProvider maps provider aliases (anthropic→claude, openai→codex) to normalized provider names.
evidence.why: Provider normalization prevents provider name drift between config and execution.

evidence.summary: buildAgentAdapterCapabilities defines provider-specific capabilities including sandbox mode, approval policy, file_write, and shell_exec per provider.
evidence.why: Provider capabilities contract documents which features each provider supports.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agent_tool_profile (boundary/research/full) and resets Claude settings, tools, and session persistence for evidence mode.
evidence.why: Evidence mode configuration isolates tool capabilities based on authorized profile level.

evidence.summary: Test verifies codex dispatch does not inherit claude default model or force read-only sandbox.
evidence.why: Evidence that provider-specific model config is isolated between providers.

evidence.summary: Test verifies claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags and relies on boundary enforcement instead of CLI tool filtering.
evidence.why: Evidence that tool capability preservation uses boundary enforcement rather than provider-side restrictions.

evidence.summary: Test verifies claude full tool profile requires explicit allowFullAgentTools authorization; defaults to boundary profile without authorization.
evidence.why: Evidence that full tool capabilities require explicit opt-in rather than being default.

evidence.summary: Test suite verifies boundary detection blocks project file mutations even when agent results pass, including git-backed detection of untracked and gitignored files.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary enforcement prevents target project mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary audit compares before/after snapshots and adds BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for any detected project changes.
evidence.why: Boundary audit converts any detected mutations into PRD readiness blockers.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier protocols with writes_code=false and evidence scope requirements.
evidence.why: Evidence agent protocol definition documents read-only constraint and scope validation requirements.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness validates evidence agent results, checks scope declarations, and blocks PRD readiness when project facts lack project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Readiness enforcement separates assumptions from facts and requires project evidence for project claims.

assumptions: Git-based boundary detection works correctly in the target project repository
Provider CLI commands (claude, codex) are available and correctly versioned in the execution environment
risks: A bug in boundary snapshot logic could fail to detect project mutations, allowing silent evidence-agent mutations
Provider-specific model config normalization could drift if new providers are added without updating PROVIDER_ALIASES
Custom provider adapter executes arbitrary commands without sandbox verification by the SDK

result.notes: Evidence dispatch system implements provider-isolated configuration with boundary enforcement and tool capability preservation. All key claims verified from source code and tests.

agent_results.claim: Cross-check verification confirms the explorer claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch system implements provider-specific configuration with boundary enforcement and tool capability preservation for Claude, Codex, and custom providers.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime independently verified: spawns provider agents, collects results, runs boundary audit, and converts mutations to PRD blockers.
evidence.why: Core dispatch implementation that executes evidence agents and enforces boundary constraints.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation independently verified: constructs isolated CLI arguments for claude, codex, and custom providers without cross-leakage.
evidence.why: Provider isolation logic prevents configuration drift between providers.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots independently verified: git-backed file tracking detects added/modified/deleted files excluding allowed artifact roots.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement implementation detects any project mutations.

evidence.summary: PROVIDER_ALIASES independently verified: maps anthropic→claude, openai→codex, shell/local→custom to normalize provider names.
evidence.why: Provider normalization prevents name drift between configuration and execution.

evidence.summary: buildAgentAdapterCapabilities independently verified: returns provider-specific sandbox, approval_policy, file_write, and shell_exec capabilities per provider.
evidence.why: Provider capabilities contract documents feature support per provider.

evidence.summary: executionConfig independently verified: normalizes agent_tool_profile to boundary/research/full, resets claude settings/tools to empty strings for evidence mode.
evidence.why: Evidence mode configuration isolates tool capabilities based on authorized profile.

evidence.summary: Test independently verified: codex dispatch asserts provider=codex, claude model is cleared, no read-only sandbox forced.
evidence.why: Evidence that provider-specific configuration is isolated.

evidence.summary: Test independently verified: claude dispatch asserts empty tools/allowedTools/disallowedTools, relies on boundary enforcement instead of CLI tool filtering.
evidence.why: Evidence that tool capability preservation uses boundary enforcement.

evidence.summary: Test independently verified: full profile defaults to boundary without allowFullAgentTools; only with explicit authorization does it become full.
evidence.why: Evidence that full tool capabilities require explicit opt-in.

evidence.summary: Test suite independently verified: blocks project file mutations, detects untracked and gitignored files, excludes allowed artifact roots from boundary violations.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary enforcement prevents target project mutations.

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary audit independently verified: compares before/after snapshots, adds BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for any detected changes.
evidence.why: Boundary audit converts detected mutations into PRD readiness blockers.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS independently verified: defines explorer, cross-checker, verifier with writes_code=false and scope validation requirements.
evidence.why: Evidence agent protocol definition documents read-only constraint.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness independently verified: validates evidence agent results, checks scope declarations, blocks when project facts lack project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Readiness enforcement separates assumptions from facts.

assumptions: Git-based boundary detection works correctly in the target project repository
Provider CLI commands (claude, codex) are available and correctly versioned in the execution environment
risks: A bug in boundary snapshot logic could fail to detect project mutations, allowing silent evidence-agent mutations
Provider-specific model config normalization could drift if new providers are added without updating PROVIDER_ALIASES
Custom provider adapter executes arbitrary commands without sandbox verification by the SDK

result.notes: All 13 evidence records from the explorer were independently verified through direct file reads. Provider isolation, boundary enforcement, and tool capability preservation claims are accurate. No conflicts found.

agent_results.claim: Verification confirms that gathered evidence is sufficient and properly structured for PRD readiness. No assumptions are presented as facts, all evidence paths are usable and verified, and missing data is properly reflected in assumptions.

evidence.summary: Verified: Core dispatch implementation exists and is correctly cited in both evidence results with identical line ranges.
evidence.why: Confirms evidence accuracy and file path validity.

evidence.summary: Verified: Provider isolation logic exists and is accurately described across both evidence results.
evidence.why: Confirms provider implementation evidence is factual.

evidence.summary: Verified: Boundary enforcement test suite exists with comprehensive coverage of mutation detection.
evidence.why: Confirms test evidence supports boundary enforcement claims.

evidence.summary: Verified: PRD readiness inspection logic exists and validates evidence scope declarations.
evidence.why: Confirms readiness enforcement mechanism evidence.

assumptions: Git-based boundary detection works correctly in the target project repository
Provider CLI commands (claude, codex) are available and correctly versioned in the execution environment
risks: If git-based boundary detection fails, evidence agents could silently mutate project files
If provider CLI commands are unavailable, evidence dispatch will fail at Runtime
A bug in boundary snapshot logic could fail to detect project mutations

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker results are properly aligned with no conflicts. All evidence records use valid project-scoped sources with accurate file paths and line numbers. Assumptions are correctly separated from facts and reflect Runtime environmental factors. Missing data is empty. Evidence is sufficient for PRD readiness.
